prompt: A rice WGS VCF record has the following genotype for sample IRIS_107: 0|1:99:42:22,20:0,500,520. The FORMAT field is GT:GQ:DP:AD:PL. Is this genotype phased or unphased? What is the genotype quality? What are the allelic depths for the reference and first alternate allele?
context:
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	IRIS_107
chr03	8842156	.	C	T	2840	PASS	AF=0.14;AN=200;AC=28	GT:GQ:DP:AD:PL	0|1:99:42:22,20:0,500,520
correct_answer: Phased (indicated by | separator); GQ=99; AD ref=22, alt=20
rubric: Must identify phased (pipe separator), GQ=99, and AD values 22 (ref) and 20 (alt). All three required for full marks.
notes: Tests recognition of phased genotype notation (pipe vs slash) plus FORMAT field extraction.
